# fcs_environment_egress (Resource)

An egress-allow rule for a cluster, applied through the central FCS NAT-GW egress ACLs (kube-ovn). All attributes force replacement; there is no in-place update. Destroy removes the rule and waits until it is reconciled away.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cluster_id` (String) ID of the cluster the rule is scoped to. Changing it forces a new egress rule.
- `destination_cidr` (String) Destination CIDR (IPv4/IPv6) or /32 host the cluster may reach (IPs and CIDRs only; no FQDNs). Changing it forces a new egress rule.
- `environment_id` (String) ID of the fcs_environment hosting the cluster. Changing it forces a new egress rule.

### Optional

- `port_range` (String) Port or port range for tcp/udp, e.g. "443" or "8000-9000". Changing it forces a new egress rule.
- `protocol` (String) Protocol: tcp | udp | icmp | any (default "any"). Changing it forces a new egress rule.
- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Server-assigned egress rule ID (UUID).
- `status` (String) Lifecycle status: provisioning | offboarding | destroyed.

<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) How long to retry temporary HTTP 503 responses from the create call (default 5m0s). Accepts a duration string such as "10m".
- `delete` (String) How long to wait for the teardown to finish (GET returns 404 or status=destroyed; default 15m0s).
